claude_reasoning.reasoning: Effective tax rate shows 250000.00 (25,000,000%) instead of expected ~0.25 (25%). This appears to be a unit conversion error where the percentage was not divided by 100.
Net income (12.4M) ≠ EBT (16.5M) - Tax expense (4.1M) = 12.4M. The math actually works correctly, suggesting the validation rule may have incorrect inputs or the expected value calculation is wrong.
claude_reasoning.suggested_fix: 12400000.00 is correct based on the calculation

claude_reasoning.reasoning: Gross margin extracted as 40900000 (absolute value) instead of ratio 0.4087. Should be gross_profit/revenue = 60.7M/148.5M ≈ 0.409
Same issue as flag 2 - gross margin should be a ratio (0-1) not absolute value
Interest payment sign convention can vary by model design. Some models track interest payments as positive outflows rather than negative expenses.
Same effective tax rate unit error as FY2022A - should be 0.25, not 250000.00
